entries.title: Validator Operations
entries.summary: Reference material for validator uptime, identity, and maintenance.

entries.title: Xahau Buildout
entries.summary: Working notes for future Xahau marketplace and AI integrations.

entries.title: Trading Fundamentals: Markets & Order Types
entries.summary: Core concepts for understanding how markets operate, order types, execution basics, and how these apply across crypto and traditional markets.
details: Describe market participants: retail, professional traders, market makers, exchanges/venues and liquidity providers.
Explain order types: market, limit, stop (stop-loss, stop-limit), IOC/FOK, and conditional orders and their execution risk.
Differentiate venue types: CEX order-book exchanges vs DEX AMMs and implications for slippage and MEV.
Outline execution considerations: latency, slippage, partial fills, order routing and best execution practices.
Summarize position management basics: leverage, margin, maintenance margin and liquidation mechanics (especially on crypto margin products).

links.label: Binance - Order Types & How Orders Work

entries.title: Technical Indicators: EMA, RSI, Fibonacci and Common Tools
entries.summary: Reference guide for widely used indicators (EMA, RSI, Fibonacci retracements) including interpretation, common settings, and pitfalls.
details: EMA: definition, calculation differences vs SMA, common periods (9, 12, 21, 50, 200), use in trend-following and cross strategies.
RSI: oscillator range (0–100), overbought/oversold rules (70/30 heuristic), divergence, and smoothing with EMA.
Fibonacci retracement: key ratios (23.6, 38.2, 50, 61.8), how to draw retracements from swing high/low, use as confluence zones with support/resistance.
Combine indicators conservatively: avoid indicator stacking without price/market-structure confirmation; prefer indicator + price action + liquidity context.
Watch for look-ahead and survivorship bias when backtesting indicator strategies.

entries.title: Liquidity & Market Structure
entries.summary: Operational framing of liquidity concepts, order book dynamics, support/resistance, supply/demand zones, and how to read market structure.
details: Define liquidity metrics: bid/ask spread, depth, volume at price levels, market impact and slippage.
Order book dynamics: understand depth, resting orders, cancellations, iceberg orders and how order flow changes price.
Market structure primitives: higher highs / higher lows (uptrend) vs lower lows / lower highs (downtrend), structure breaks as trigger signals.
Liquidity zones: cluster significant limit order levels, previous high-volume nodes, and on-chain token holder concentration for crypto.
Use combined data: time & sales (tape), order book snapshots and VWAP to assess short-term execution risk.

links.label: TradingView - Volume Profile & Market Structure tools

entries.title: Market Makers, Whales & Flow Analysis
entries.summary: How market makers and large holders ('whales') affect price discovery, liquidity provision, and manipulative patterns in crypto markets.
details: Role of market makers: provide two-sided quotes, reduce spreads, may have inventory and risk limits — understand incentives and times when they withdraw liquidity.
Detect whale flows: large transfers between wallets/exchanges and on-chain jumps can presage price moves or liquidity drains.
Watch for manipulation vectors: spoofing, wash trading, concentrated sell-offs or wash bids on low-liquidity pairs (common in some spot/alt markets).
On-chain whale tracking: monitor large transfers to exchanges, exchange inflows/outflows and known wallet labels for probability of sell-side pressure.
Combine on-chain signals with order book and exchange flow for higher-confidence hypotheses about imminent moves.

links.label: Whale Alert - large crypto transfer tracker
Nansen - Wallet labeling and flow analytics

entries.title: Crypto-Specific On-Chain Metrics & Analytics
entries.summary: Key on-chain signals and data sources critical to crypto trading: exchange flows, active addresses, supply movement, staking/locking, and protocol-specific dynamics.
details: Exchange flows: inflows to exchanges often precede selling pressure; outflows can indicate accumulation or cold storage.
Network activity: active addresses, transactions per day, unique senders/receivers as adoption/usage proxies.
Supply changes: unlocking schedules, staking rewards, vesting cliffs, and token burns materially change available float.
DEX / AMM-specific: TVL, liquidity pools composition, impermanent loss risk and slippage for large trades.
XRPL specifics: on-ledger DEX, pathfinding, and trustlines — monitor ledger transactions and orderbook offers for XRPL-native signals.

entries.title: Trading Strategies & Risk Management
entries.summary: Common strategy archetypes (momentum, mean reversion, scalping, swing), with practical risk-management rules for crypto markets.
details: Strategy archetypes: momentum (trend-following), mean reversion (counter-trend), breakout/fade, arbitrage, and liquidity-taking strategies.
Timeframes & matching tools: align strategy horizon (intraday, swing, position) with indicators and execution tools to avoid mismatch.
Risk rules: define per-trade risk (percentage of capital), max drawdown limits, use of stop-loss vs hedging and use of position sizing (fixed fractional, Kelly variants).
Backtesting & out-of-sample forward testing are mandatory; use realistic slippage, fees and market-impact models especially for illiquid pairs.
Psychology & operational hygiene: plan for edge decay, overnight risks, exchange counterparty risk and maintain a reproducible trade log for audits.

entries.title: Trading Tools, Libraries & Data Sources
entries.summary: Practical tools and APIs for market data, execution, backtesting and on-chain analytics useful for research and production trading systems.
details: Exchange APIs & wrappers: CCXT for unified REST/WebSocket across many CEXs; direct exchange native APIs for advanced features and raw orderbook access.
Backtesting & research: vectorbt, Backtrader, Backtesting.py and QuantConnect for strategy development and historical simulation.
On-chain data & analytics: Glassnode, Nansen, Dune Analytics, Etherscan APIs, and blockchain explorers (XRPL explorers for XRPL).
Market data & aggregators: TradingView for charts/indicators, CoinGecko and CoinMarketCap APIs for basic pricing and meta info.
Execution & infra: colocated/low-latency requirements for HFT, careful rate-limit handling for APIs, and logging/trade-reconciliation for auditability.

entries.title: Execution Best Practices & Production Considerations
entries.summary: Operational checklist for moving from research to live execution including monitoring, safety, and auditability.
details: Start small: run paper or small-dollar live tests with full logging before scaling capital; ensure end-to-end reconciliation.
Monitoring & alerts: order fill monitoring, PnL tracking, exchange connectivity, and on-call escalation playbooks for outages or severe slippage.
Rate limits & retries: implement exponential backoff, idempotency for order placement/cancellation and robust error handling.
Security & compliance: protect API keys, use least-privilege exchange keys, maintain segregated accounts for production and testing.
Reproducibility & audit logs: persist raw market data, orderbook snapshots and full trade lifecycle for debugging and compliance.

entries.title: XRPL & Xahau Context for Trading
entries.summary: Notes tying XRPL-specific mechanics and the Xahau buildout into trading considerations and data flows for this workspace.
details: XRPL has an on-ledger DEX and native orderbook offers: monitor ledger offers, pathfinding and trustline exposures for token liquidity analysis.
Use XRPL explorers and APIs to track offers, account balances and cross-currency path liquidity for trade planning.
Integrate Xahau buildout notes: map Xahau marketplace objectives to XRPL data sources (orderbook, offers, ledger events) and agent brain signals.
For XRPL-specific trading, consider automated watch for large offers/offers cancellations and on-ledger payment flows for whale detection.
Documented endpoints: use xrpl.org APIs and local Xahau artifacts in the workspace as primary sources for XRPL implementation details.

entries.title: XRPL Core: Ledger APIs, Pathfinding, AMM & Gateways
entries.summary: Authoritative XRPL reference material for ledger interaction, orderbooks, on-ledger AMM, pathfinding (auto-bridging via offers), gateways and rippled API usage.
details: Primary developer docs: use xrpl.org as the canonical reference for ledger primitives, AMM, pathfinding, escrows and multisign behavior.
Ledger interaction: prefer xrpl.js (Node) or xrpl-py (Python) clients and rippled JSON-RPC / WebSocket subscriptions for production integration.
Orderbook & pathfinding: rely on book_offers, account_offers and path_find (pathfinding) mechanisms to implement auto-bridging and routed swaps across offers.
AMM on XRPL: review XRPL AMM design and on-ledger AMM primitives to understand pools, liquidity provider accounting and fee flows before automating LP operations.
Gateways & bridging model: treat gateways as off-ledger custodial bridges; combine gateway monitoring (ledger deposits/withdrawals) with pathfinding to detect liquidity routes and bridge flows.
Operational tips: subscribe to ledger notifications (validated ledgers, account_tx), persist orderbook snapshots and path quotes for reconciliation and slippage modelling.

entries.title: Xahau Buildout & Workspace Integration
entries.summary: Local workspace notes and the Xahau buildout reference to map marketplace objectives to XRPL data and agent signals.
details: Primary workspace link: reference the local Xahau buildout entry for project-specific integration details and objectives.
Map Xahau features to XRPL primitives: identify which features map to XRPL offers, AMM pools, escrow/multisig and pathfinding routes.
Agent brain signals: define which ledger events (offer create/cancel, AMM liquidity changes, gateway deposits) will feed the agent brain for alerts and automation.
Implementation checklist: ensure credentials and keys remain protected (do not store in repo), and design testnet runs (Songbird/Sandbox) before mainnet operations.

entries.title: EVM Sidechain Patterns: Flare, F-Assets & State Connector
entries.summary: Resources describing Flare's approach to bringing EVM functionality tied to XRPL/XRP via F-Assets, the State Connector and canonical bridge patterns used by EVM sidechains anchored to XRPL assets.
details: Flare fundamentals: review Flare's documentation to understand State Connector, Spark (FLR) mechanics and how F-Assets (e.g., FXRP) represent XRPL assets on an EVM environment.
Sidechain anchoring pattern: study F-Asset mint/burn lifecycle, oracle or state-connector attestations, and security assumptions for pegged assets.
Testnets & tooling: use Songbird (Flare testnet) to prototype cross-chain flows, F-Asset minting and smart-contract interactions in an EVM environment.
Data & monitoring: monitor Flare nodes, state-connector logs and F-Asset mint/burn transactions for bridge reconciliations and forensic audits.

entries.title: Flare DeFi: Ecosystem & Data Sources
entries.summary: Practical data sources and integration touchpoints for tracking DeFi activity on Flare and Songbird, including protocol repositories, block explorers and community-maintained analytics.
details: Official sources: prioritize Flare Docs and the Flare GitHub to identify protocol contracts, governance documents and technical releases.
Explorer & node data: use Songbird/Flare explorers and RPC endpoints to fetch transactions, contract events and block history for reconciliation and analytics.
Ecosystem discovery: catalogue DeFi projects from official Flare ecosystem pages and GitHub to track AMMs, bridges and vault products available on the network.
Onboarding feeds: subscribe to protocol release notes, project GitHub repos and community channels to detect upgrades, token listings and security advisories.

entries.title: XRPL AMM, Vaults & Multisig Operational Notes
entries.summary: Focused notes on how XRPL AMMs operate, recommended vault/multisig patterns on XRPL, and ledger-level primitives to secure multi-sign vaults and LP positions.
details: AMM mechanics: review pool lifecycle, liquidity provider share accounting and fee distribution as specified in XRPL AMM docs before implementing LP automation or treasury flows.
Vault & multisig patterns: combine XRPL multisign features, escrow and regular on-ledger offers to build multisig vaults and timelocked release patterns.
Monitoring & reconciliation: track AMM pool_offer events, LP token/account changes and use ledger indexes to tie liquidity changes to signed transactions for audits.
Access control: apply least-privilege for operational keys, use multisign thresholds and signer lists; persist signer configuration snapshots for incident response.

entries.title: Auto-bridging & Pathfinding on XRPL (Practical Integration)
entries.summary: Operational reference for implementing auto-bridging on XRPL using the ledger's pathfinding, orderbook composition and gateway models; includes APIs and monitoring guidance.
details: Auto-bridging concept: use XRPL pathfinding to programmatically route a source asset to a destination asset across offers and gateways (XRPL path_find and book_offers workflows).

Implementation recipe: 1) query book_offers for direct liquidity; 2) use path_find for multi-hop quotes; 3) simulate slippage and fees; 4) place offer/payment with explicit paths or allow ripple pathing.
Gateway considerations: monitor gateway deposit/withdrawal ledgers and treat gateway solvency signals as part of routing/credit risk decisions.
Operational monitoring: persist path snapshots and chosen offers at time of execution, log fill partials and cancellations; alert on rapid offer changes or gateway anomalies.

entries.title: Agent Design Patterns: High-Quality Agent Brain
entries.summary: Core architectural patterns and components for building robust trading agents: signal ingestion, feature store, decision engine, risk overlay, execution adapter, and feedback loops.
details: Decompose the agent into clear layers: Data Ingestion → Feature Store → Signal Engine / Models → Decision Engine → Risk Manager → Execution Adapter → Audit & Replay.
Signal sources: separate fast (orderbook, trades) and slow (on-chain events, fundamentals) sources; normalize into time-aligned feature feeds.
Feature store: durable, immutable time-series store with versioning and labeling for backtests and online inference (Parquet/TSDB + metadata).
Decision engine: deterministic policy layers (ensemble of heuristics + ML scoring) with explicit priority rules and a transparent tie-breaker.
Risk overlay: enforce per-trade and portfolio-level constraints (max exposure, leverage limits, stop-loss envelope, position concentration) as last gating step before orders.
Execution adapter: abstract exchange/ledger specifics behind adapters that implement idempotency keys, retries, rate-limit handling and reconciliation hooks.
Feedback & learning loop: persist outcomes (fills, slippage, partials) to feed model retraining and parameter tuning; mark events for manual review when anomalies occur.
Explainability & audit: log decision context for every action (input signals, model outputs, effective constraints) to enable post-mortem and compliance reviews.
Operational modes: explicit modes for paper, canary (small live capital), and production — each using the same codepaths but different config and kill-switch thresholds.
Separation of concerns: keep deterministic execution code separate from experimental ML code; require feature-flagged rollout and staged promotion to production.

entries.title: Concrete Open-Source Trading Bot Repositories (examples)
entries.summary: Practical references: existing trading bot projects that illustrate filesystem layout, config models, strategies, execution layers and community guardrails.
details: Hummingbot: modular architecture showing connectors, strategies, client UI and deployment patterns — useful for designing exchange adapters and strategy abstractions.
Freqtrade: clear separation of strategies, backtesting, hyperopt and live modes; includes configuration profiles, paper trading and CI-friendly test harnesses.
CCXT: canonical unified API adapter used as a reference for exchange adapter implementations and consistent error/rate-limit handling.
Zenbot / Gekko / other historical projects: examples of end-to-end scripts, lightweight folder layouts and trade-off lessons about maintenance/technical debt in long-running bots.
Auto-agent examples (Auto-GPT / LangChain): illustrate orchestration patterns, tool use, and guardrails for autonomous agents — adapt orchestration patterns, not raw LLM-driven autonomy, for trading.

entries.title: Recommended Repository & Filesystem Layouts for Trading Agents
entries.summary: Opinionated, operationally-productive repo layouts and file conventions to support development, testing, and secure deployment of trading agents.
details: Top-level layout: README, LICENSE, docker-compose.yml, infra/, ci/, deployments/, docs/, src/, tests/, data/, tools/, configs/.
src/ structure: src/adapters/ (exchange/ledger connectors), src/engine/ (decision & risk), src/strategies/, src/sim/ (simulator & backtest runner), src/services/ (indexer, ingestion), src/cli/.
configs/: environment-specific YAML/JSON (paper, canary, prod) with explicit feature flags; never commit secrets — use placeholders and secret-injection at deploy time.
data/: store historical market data, labeled feature snapshots, and canonical backtest artifacts; keep large data outside repo or referenced via pointers to artifact storage.
infra/: deployment manifests (k8s, docker), service definitions for node/indexer, and infra-as-code for observability (prometheus, grafana dashboards).
ci/: automated test suites, backtest regression checks, linting, and reproducible environment matrix definitions; require unit + integration coverage gates.
scripts/: utilities for dataset ingestion, ledger indexer bootstrapping, snapshot exports and replay drivers (replay market data into simulator).
docs/: architecture diagrams, runbooks, on-call procedures, and developer guides for onboarding new strategies or connectors.

entries.title: Guardrails & Safety Loops for Trading Agents
entries.summary: Practical guardrails: safety controls, kill switches, audits, and human-in-the-loop patterns to prevent catastrophic loss and to enable auditable automated operations.
details: Multi-tier safety: pre-order validation (syntax & risk checks), pre-execution gating (circuit breakers), post-execution reconciliation (fills vs expected), and human escalation triggers.
Circuit breakers: global (halt all trading on large drawdown or outage), strategy-level (suspend if edge decays), and exchange-adapter-level (retry/backoff when rate limits or errors spike).
Per-trade risk checks: max notional, max qty, allowed currency pairs list, min liquidity thresholds, and simple slippage bounds before accepting fills.
Idempotency & ordinality: use idempotency keys for each logical order, persist external/order-provider ids and reconcile to guard against duplicates on retries.
Access & secrets: never store API keys in repo; use secrets manager (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) and least-privilege keys; maintain separate keys for paper/canary/prod.
Human-in-the-loop gates: require manual approval for high-impact operations (large treasury moves, parameter changes above thresholds, deploy critical upgrades).
Observability & alerting: critical alerts for gateway insolvency, exchange outages, unexpected filler rates, and reconciliation failures; include automated escalation playbooks.
Operational logs & audit: immutable append-only logs for orders, cancels, fills and ledger events; store raw market snapshots used for each decision for reproducible investigations.
Sim-to-live parity: ensure production adapters reuse same codepath as simulator with dependency injection for data feeds to reduce unforeseen divergence.

entries.title: Testing, Deployment & Observability for Trading Agents
entries.summary: Testing strategy (backtest, replay, integration), deployment patterns (canary, feature flags), and monitoring stacks (metrics, tracing, error reporting) tuned for trading operations.
details: Testing pyramid: unit tests for strategy primitives, component tests for adapters & risk, integration tests against sandbox exchanges, full system backtest/regression suites.
Historical replay: ship a deterministic replay engine that can inject recorded orderbook & trades into live-like components for regression and incident replay.
Backtest hygiene: enforce realistic slippage, fees, latency models and hidden-data controls (no lookahead); maintain an out-of-sample period and rolling forward-tests.
CI/CD: run backtest/regression checks in CI for PRs that touch engine code; block merges on failing regression or reduced edge beyond accepted thresholds.
Deployment patterns: immutable deployments (containers), canary releases with gradual traffic ramp and configurable kill-switch; use feature flags for experimental models.
Observability: instrument key metrics (latency, fills, slippage, PnL per strategy, open orders, exchange health) and trace critical flows; integrate alerting with runbooks.
Error handling & retries: exponential backoff for API errors, circuit breaker pattern for high-error rates, and store failed operations for manual reconciliation.
Incident response: runbooks for exchange outage, reconciliation mismatch, gateway insolvency and leaked keys; include contact lists and required remediation steps.

entries.title: XRPL & Xahau Integration Patterns for Agent Builders
entries.summary: Practical patterns to integrate XRPL ledger features and Xahau objectives into agents: ledger subscriptions, pathfinding, gateway monitoring, and indexer design.
details: Primary sources: use xrpl.org docs and xrpl.js/xrpl-py clients for canonical interaction patterns (ledger subscribe, account_tx, book_offers, path_find).
Indexer pattern: run a local indexer that persists orderbook snapshots, offers changes, AMM events and gateway deposit/withdraw events for fast queries and reconciliation.
Pathfinding & auto-bridging: query book_offers first for direct liquidity, then use path_find for multi-hop routes; persist path snapshots chosen at execution time for audit.
Gateway health & credit risk: monitor gateway inflows/outflows and labeled wallet activity to detect solvency or bridge risk that should influence routing.
AMM / LP operations: treat AMM pool changes as ledger events, record LP token/account changes, and gate LP automation behind multisig/time-delay patterns for treasury safety.
Simulations: replay ledger events into simulator and validate path_find outcomes against historical fills to calibrate slippage & fee assumptions.
Xahau mapping: model Xahau marketplace features to XRPL primitives (offers, AMM pools, escrows) and define agent brain signals that reflect marketplace objectives.
Operational infra: keep rippled access behind internal adapters, rate-limit rippled calls, and provide local caching to reduce latency and load on public nodes.

entries.title: XRPL Wallet Connection: Secure Integration for Trading Bot
entries.summary: Operational recipe to connect an XRPL account to the bot backend, validate balance, subscribe to ledger events and expose a server-side status endpoint while keeping private keys out of the repo.
details: Never commit private seeds or secret keys to the repo. Use a secrets manager or environment variables injected at deploy time (example env var: XRPL_WALLET_SEED).
Prefer server-side wallet handling only (do not hand seeds to browser clients). Derive public address from seed server-side and persist only the public address in configs if needed.

Use xrpl.js (Node) or xrpl-py (Python) in server code (choose xrpl.js if server.js is Node). Example flow: load seed from secret store -> Wallet.fromSeed(seed) -> connect to rippled client -> request account_info for balance.

Implementation steps (server-side): 1) Load RIPPLED_URL and XRPL_WALLET_SEED from secrets; 2) Connect to xrpl client and await connect(); 3) Derive classic address from seed; 4) Call account_info to read XRP balance and sequence; 5) Subscribe to account_tx or ledger stream for live updates; 6) Implement retry/backoff and idempotency for requests.
Expose a minimal JSON endpoint for the dashboard (e.g. GET /api/admin/wallet/status) that returns { address, balanceDrops, balanceXRP, lastLedger, connected } and hides any secret fields.
Persist balance snapshots (timestamped) to the data store for history/audit (store location outside repo; reference in configs/data path).
For testing use XRPL Testnet or a local rippled; never initially perform treasury moves on mainnet without multisig/time-delay and manual operator approval.

entries.title: Admin Wallet Dashboard: Owner-Only UI & Backend Status Endpoint
entries.summary: Design and implementation checklist to add an owner-authenticated dashboard (admin-projects/) showing the connected XRPL wallet address, online connection status, latest balance and recent transactions.
details: Place UI under admin-projects/wallet-dashboard/ and ensure route is owner-only per workspace guardrails. Do not make this page public.
Server endpoint(s): implement GET /api/admin/wallet/status (returns address, balanceXRP, lastLedger, connected boolean, lastSyncTs); implement GET /api/admin/wallet/txs (recent tx history) if needed.
Server implementation must read the wallet seed from secrets only and preserve existing owner authentication and whitelist logic in server.js when adding endpoints.

UI elements: connection indicator (green/yellow/red), public address, formatted XRP balance, last synced ledger/time, recent transactions table (direction, amount, ledger, memo), manual refresh button and auto-refresh toggle.
Realtime updates: optional websockets or SSE from server to push account_tx events into the UI; fallback to periodic polling (e.g. every 10s) with rate-limit safeguards.

Safety features: Add a configurable low-balance alert (env WALLET_MIN_BALANCE_ALERT), and a visible note that spending requires offline/owner approval. Disable any key-entry or signing capability from the UI.
Audit & logs: record each API call and wallet status snapshot to an append-only audit log for post-mortem and reconciliations.
Developer test mode: support a paper/test mode (configurable) that reads from testnet and labels the dashboard accordingly.

entries.title: Secrets & Safety: Handling XRPL Seeds and Wallet Credentials
entries.summary: Practical guardrails and recommended tooling for storing and using wallet seeds safely in this workspace to prevent accidental leakage and to maintain auditable operations.
details: Do not store seeds in repo files (.git), docs or public pages. The workspace protects .env and .data/ but also avoid checking plaintext placeholders containing real seeds.
Use a secrets manager (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager) or host-level encrypted keystore. Inject secrets into runtime via CI/CD or container secret mounts.

When a seed is operator-provided (as in the task block), treat it as sensitive: instruct operator to store it in secret manager and set XRPL_WALLET_SEED in runtime, or put it only on an operator machine outside the repo.
For treasury or significant-value wallets prefer multisig/time-lock patterns on XRPL rather than single-seed operational keys. Keep single-seed wallets for test/paper/canary only.
Access control: limit which service accounts and operator keys can read secrets, rotate seeds regularly and audit all secret reads/uses. Log operations that used the wallet for transactions.
If a seed is ever exposed, treat the wallet as compromised: immediately create a new wallet, drain funds via safe multisig-controlled flows if possible, and follow incident response playbook.

entries.title: XRPL Trading Interface (admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot)
entries.summary: Project spec and file-layout for an owner-only XRPL trading-bot UI placed under admin-projects. Tracks XRPL, Xahau, Flare and EVM-sidechain metrics, themed red/black, and built to observe (not sign) until wallet secrets are supplied via secrets manager.
details: Target path: admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/ (new folder; owner-only route per workspace guardrails).

Files to create: index.html (UI), client.js (client-side logic), style.css (local; red/black theme), assets/ (icons, logos), README.md (developer notes). Do NOT modify top-level index.html, script.js, style.css or other protected files.

UI design: dark-red (#8B0000) + black theme; prominent red action buttons styled consistently (no signing UI visible); connection indicator (green/yellow/red), public address, formatted XRP balance, last ledger/time, recent transactions table, metrics panels (XRPL offers, AMM liquidity, Xahau tokens, Flare F-Assets, EVM sidechain balances), manual refresh and auto-refresh toggle.

Metrics to surface (minimum): XRP wallet balance (drops & XRP), recent account_tx (direction, amount, ledger, memo), open offers/book_offers snapshots, AMM pool liquidity & TVL (XRPL AMM), path_find snapshots & selected route, Xahau token metrics (listed tokens and on-ledger offers), Flare F-Asset balances and Songbird testnet indicators, EVM-sidechain account balances for pegged assets, PnL & recent fills (derived), slippage & last-execution snapshot.
Data update model: prefer server-pushed events (SSE/WebSocket) for account_tx and ledger changes; fallback to polling (e.g. 5–10s) with configurable rate limits to avoid public node overload.

Storage & audit: persist timestamped snapshots and audit logs outside the repo (external data path or secret-configured storage). UI shows latest snapshot but raw snapshots written to configured datastore for replay and post-mortem.
Test mode: default to XRPL Testnet / Songbird for development. UI should display network label (TESTNET / MAINNET) prominently.
No secrets in UI: never accept or display private seeds in the browser. The UI is observation-only until operator-supplied seed is injected server-side via secrets manager.

Project listing: add an owner-only link entry under admin-projects index/listing (internal projects listing page) to make the page navigable from admin projects; do NOT alter public navigation or protected files.

entries.title: Server Endpoints & Secrets Handling for XRPL Trading Interface
entries.summary: Server-side recipe and required endpoints for XRPL connectivity, safe seed handling, telemetry endpoints and owner-auth preservation when wiring the trading interface to rippled via xrpl.js.
details: Use xrpl.js (Node) on the server. Prefer Node if server.js is Node-based; otherwise note xrpl-py alternative. Do not store seeds in repo.
Secrets: load XRPL_WALLET_SEED and RIPPLED_URL from secrets manager or environment injection at runtime. Never log seeds or write them to repo files. Use Vault/AWS Secrets Manager recommended.
Preserve authentication: any new endpoints must reuse existing owner authentication and owner-wallet whitelist in server.js; do NOT weaken access control or expose endpoints publicly.

Recommended endpoints (owner-only): GET /api/admin/trading/status -> { address, balanceDrops, balanceXRP, lastLedger, connected }, GET /api/admin/trading/metrics -> latest metrics snapshot, GET /api/admin/trading/txs?limit= -> recent tx list, GET /api/admin/trading/path?suggest -> persist chosen path snapshot. Optional POST /api/admin/trading/refresh to trigger immediate refresh (owner-only).

Server flow for XRPL connection: 1) load XRPL_WALLET_SEED from secret store; 2) Wallet.fromSeed(seed) -> derive address; 3) connect to rippled client (RIPPLED_URL) with retry/backoff; 4) request account_info and subscribe to account_tx and ledger streams; 5) publish sanitized status to owner-only endpoints / SSE or WebSocket.

No server-side signing until operator provides wallet seed and manual operator confirmation. If signing endpoints are later added, enforce multisig/time-delay + manual approval and require high-sensitivity audit logs.

Rate-limiting & caching: cache book_offers and path_find snapshots for a short TTL to avoid hammering public nodes. Implement exponential backoff on rippled errors and keep idempotency for refresh requests.
Audit & logs: write append-only logs for every server action involving the wallet and any metric snapshots. Persist ledger-indexed snapshots for replayability and reconciliation.
Storage: snapshots and logs should be saved to configured external storage (S3, object store, or a local data path outside repo) referenced by config; avoid writing into protected .data/ in repo.

links.label: Workspace server.js (reference for preserving owner auth)

entries.title: Operational Guardrails: Safety, Testing & Deployment for XRPL Trading Interface
entries.summary: Operational and security guardrails to follow when implementing and running the XRPL trading interface, including testnet-first, secrets handling, and auditability.
details: Testnet-first: develop and validate all features on XRPL Testnet and Songbird (Flare testnet). UI must display network mode clearly.
No secrets in repo: never commit seeds or actual private data. Use secrets manager and inject XRPL_WALLET_SEED at runtime only.
Owner-only routes: host UI under admin-projects and guard with owner auth. Do not modify public site files or global nav.
Read-only vs signing: default interface is observational/read-only. Introduce signing only after explicit operator approval and with multisig/time-delay patterns.
Observability: instrument metrics (connection health, last ledger, snapshot latency, number of offers tracked, last path_find result, snapshot errors) and surface critical alerts in admin UI.
Reproducibility: persist raw snapshots (orderbook, path quotes, account_tx) for replay engines and post-mortem debugging.
Incident playbook: include a runbook for compromised seed (rotate, freeze, move funds via multisig if available), rippled outage, and gateway insolvency detection.
Privacy & throttling: do not publish any identifiable operator data in UI. Throttle client polling to avoid overloading internal/external nodes.

entries.title: Connected XRPL Wallet: rVeHp61gJ9MxoqMkAMmEbow8KYWk3AH1X (integration-ready)
entries.summary: Canonical record for the workspace owner wallet (public address and starting balance). Implementation notes to integrate this address into the owner-only dashboard without committing secrets.
details: Public address recorded for workspace operations: rVeHp61gJ9MxoqMkAMmEbow8KYWk3AH1X. Do NOT store or commit private seed material in repo or docs.
Operator reported starting funds: 50 XRP available at the address (treat as starting-capital snapshot).
Secret handling: place the wallet seed in a secrets manager (Vault/AWS Secrets Manager/GCP Secret Manager) or inject via runtime env var on the host. Do NOT write seed into .env or repo files.
Runtime env expectations: XRPL_WALLET_SEED (secret, not committed) + RIPPLED_URL (rippled RPC/WebSocket endpoint) provided to server process at startup by secret injection.

Server responsibilities: load seed from secret store at runtime, derive classic address server-side, connect to rippled client, call account_info for balance, and subscribe to account_tx or ledger streams for live updates.

API surface (owner-only): implement GET /api/admin/wallet/status -> { address, balanceDrops, balanceXRP, lastLedger, connected, lastSyncTs } and optional GET /api/admin/wallet/txs for recent transactions.

UI placement: add observation-only wallet UI under admin-projects/wallet-dashboard/ (or admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/ dashboard panel). Show connection indicator, public address, formatted XRP balance, last ledger/time, and recent tx table.
Audit & persistence: persist timestamped balance snapshots and account_tx raw snapshots to external storage (S3/object store/local data path outside repo) for replay and auditability.
Safety: dashboard must never accept or display the private seed. Disable client-side signing; server-side signing only after explicit operator approval and enforced multisig/time-delay patterns.
Alerting: add a configurable low-balance alert env var (WALLET_MIN_BALANCE_ALERT) and surface alerts in admin UI and server logs.
Public address: rVeHp61gJ9MxoqMkAMmEbow8KYWk3AH1X. Treat this as the canonical on-repo public identifier for the owner wallet.
Starting snapshot: operator-funded starting balance recorded as 50 XRP. Use this as an initial validation value in integration tests and dashboard displays.
Do NOT commit or persist the private seed in repo files, docs, or UI. Treat any operator-provided seed as high-sensitivity secret and require storage in a secrets manager.

Runtime expectations: XRPL_WALLET_SEED (secret, not committed) + RIPPLED_URL must be injected into the server runtime via secret manager or host secret injection; server derives public address at runtime and validates it matches the recorded public address.

Dashboard integration: surface the public address and formatted balance on admin-projects wallet/trading dashboards with connection status and last-sync timestamp; always hide seeds and signing controls unless operator explicitly enables them server-side under multisig/time-delay controls.

Audit & persistence: write timestamped balance snapshots and account_tx raw snapshots to external storage (S3/object-store or host data path outside repo) for replay and auditability; do not write secrets to those stores unencrypted.

entries.title: Wallet Integration Implementation Checklist (server + dashboard)
entries.summary: Concrete operational checklist to wire the recorded XRPL wallet into the trading-bot backend and owner-only dashboard while preserving owner authentication and secrets safety.
details: 1) Secrets: Put the provided seed into a secrets manager and set XRPL_WALLET_SEED in runtime via secret injection. Do NOT commit seed. Document secrets path and access controls in ops runbook.
2) Config: Ensure RIPPLED_URL is configured (public node or internal rippled) and injected at runtime. Add WALLET_MIN_BALANCE_ALERT as optional env var.
3) server.js: Add owner-only endpoints reusing existing auth/owner-wallet whitelist. Preserve all existing authentication logic — do not weaken access control.

4) XRPL connection (server-side using xrpl.js): load seed from secret store -> Wallet.fromSeed(seed) -> derive address; connect to rippled client with retry/backoff; fetch account_info for initial balance; subscribe to account_tx/ledger streams.

5) API contract: GET /api/admin/wallet/status returns sanitized object { address, balanceDrops, balanceXRP, lastLedger, connected, lastSyncTs } (no secrets). Optional GET /api/admin/wallet/txs?limit= for recent txs and POST /api/admin/wallet/refresh (owner-only) to force refresh.
6) Caching & rate-limits: cache book_offers/path_find snapshots; rate-limit polling from UI; implement exponential backoff for rippled errors.

7) Persistence & audit: write append-only logs for each snapshot and API call; persist snapshots to configured external storage (S3 or local data path outside repo). Capture ledger-indexed snapshots for replay.

8) UI (admin-projects/wallet-dashboard/): build read-only page showing connection indicator, public address, formatted balance, last ledger/time, recent tx table, manual refresh button, auto-refresh toggle; mark network label (TESTNET/MAINNET).
9) UI safety: never accept seed input in browser; display clear notice that spending requires owner approval and server-side secrets; support Testnet mode for development.

10) Deploy & test: inject secrets on staging/test host, start server, verify GET /api/admin/wallet/status shows correct public address and balance, verify dashboard shows connection and 50 XRP starting balance, confirm snapshots written to external storage.
11) Incident & ops: add runbook entry for compromised seed (rotate/replace wallet), rippled outage, and gateway insolvency detection; log and alert critical anomalies.

entries.title: XRPL Trading Strategies: Concrete Ideas & Implementation Notes
entries.summary: A catalog of implementable trading strategies tuned for XRPL primitives (offers, path_find, AMMs, gateways) with operational steps, safety controls and monitoring requirements so the owner-only agent can run/watch them in the admin interface.
details: RSI Mean-Reversion (XRP <-> rUSD): prerequisites: trustline to rUSD issuer and issuer solvency check. Signal: compute RSI on aggregated XRPL price candles (or external candles). Execution: when RSI > overbought threshold, place sell offer / payment path to convert a capped % of XRP to rUSD via path_find or book_offers; when RSI < oversold threshold, place buy-back offer. Risk controls: per-trade max notional (e.g., 1–3% wallet), cooldown between roundtrips, hard stop-loss in XRP terms, issuer credit limit check and liquidity threshold before placing orders. Monitoring: record pre-execution path snapshot, chosen offers, fill partials and slippage; attach ledger index for replay.

Orderbook Market-Making (small cap issued token pairs): prerequisites: trustlines, whitelist allowed issuers, and min depth heuristic. Strategy: maintain two-sided offers around mid using dynamic spread that widens during volatility or low depth; inventory skewing to keep target holdings. Execution: use book_offers and account_offers to place and cancel offers idempotently; update quotes on orderbook change events. Risk controls: max inventory exposure, circuit-breaker if fills exceed expected slippage, dynamic spread floor, and temporary withdraw on gateway anomaly. Monitoring: publish per-market quotes, inventory, and realized spread; log cancellations and fills for audit.

Path-Find Multi-Hop Arbitrage / Auto-Bridge Capture: prerequisites: index of gateway issuers, trustlines as required by target hops. Detection: scan book_offers and run path_find simulation between source/destination across candidate issuers and hops; compute net expected output after fees/slippage. Execution: only execute if simulated net > threshold after fees and risk buffers. Risk controls: require snapshot locking (persist chosen path), minimum on-ledger depth, and emergency abort if any offer in path disappears before submission. Monitoring: store path snapshots, outcome vs simulation, timestamped ledger index and reconcile fills.

AMM Opportunistic LP Management (XRPL AMM pools): prerequisites: support for XRPL AMM pools and multisig vault patterns for treasury LP actions. Strategy: add liquidity when pool TVL low relative to historic range and expected fees outweigh IL risk; remove liquidity when IL risk threshold exceeded or yields drop. Execution: call AMM deposit/withdraw transactions from server-side adapter. Risk controls: multisig gating for large moves, max % of treasury in LP, time-delays for large withdrawals, and pre-flight simulation of pool share changes. Monitoring: LP token/account snapshots, pool fee accrual and TVL time-series.

Gateway Spread Capture & Peg Arbitrage (issued stablecoins): prerequisites: known gateway issuer list, trustlines to target stablecoins, and gateway on/off-chain spread visibility. Strategy: capture spread between direct gateway buy/sell rates and orderbook offers by creating crossing offers when profitable. Execution: small tick-size orders to avoid moving market, require immediate fill or cancel. Risk controls: issuer solvency checks, max exposure per issuer, and throttle frequency to avoid being used for wash trading. Monitoring: track gateway inflows/outflows, unusual issuer behavior and reconciliation of settled issued tokens.

Volatility-Breakout with Volume Confirmation (XRP pairs): prerequisites: robust trade/tape feed or aggregated candles from orderbook snapshots. Strategy: detect structural breaks (HH/HL or breakout of consolidation) with accompanying volume surge and on-book depth removal; scale into momentum position and ladder exits. Execution: use staged limit orders or market with slippage cap; require pre-defined stop-loss and trailing stop rules. Risk controls: position-size cap, volatility-adaptive limit windows, and cancel-all on network instability. Monitoring: trade book snapshots at entry/exit, expected vs actual slippage, and per-trade telemetry.

Reactive Whale-Flow Hedging & Rebalancing: prerequisites: ledger monitoring for large transfers to/from gateway/known exchange addresses and labeling of addresses. Strategy: when large exchange inflow observed for a token, pre-emptively hedge or reduce exposure in that token; when large outflow (accumulation) observed, consider re-entry. Execution: read account_tx, exchange/gateway inflow analytics and trigger hedges via offers or cross-currency paths. Risk controls: require multi-signal confirmation (on-chain + orderbook + price move), limit number of reactive trades per window, and cap exposure per event. Monitoring: event correlation logs and outcome reconciliation.

Conservative Rebalance Engine for Multi-Asset Wallets: prerequisites: inventory tracking of all trust-lined issued assets, per-asset target allocations. Strategy: periodic rebalancing to target weights using path_find to choose low-slippage routes; prefer low-fee windows and consolidate via XRP or stable issued token with trusted issuer. Execution: schedule rebalances in low-volume windows, break into micro-trades to minimize market impact. Risk controls: maximum daily reposition notional, allowlist allowed issuers, and fail-safe if path slippage exceeds threshold. Monitoring: scheduled snapshot of holdings and rebalancing PnL/fees.

entries.title: Strategy Monitoring System: Admin-Projects Implementation Checklist & API Contract
entries.summary: Concrete server + UI design and API contract to surface active XRPL trading strategies in an owner-only admin interface (read-only observation until server-side secrets are provided). File- and endpoint-level guidance to preserve owner auth and secrets safety.
details: UI placement & access: place pages under admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/strategies/ (owner-only route per guardrails). Do NOT modify public top-level files. UI must be observation-only; no client-side seed entry or signing controls.

Server endpoints (owner-only, preserve existing auth/whitelist): GET /api/admin/trading/strategies -> list of strategy descriptors and config (id, name, enabled boolean, mode: paper/canary/prod), GET /api/admin/trading/strategies/status -> live states array { strategy_id, active, positionSummary, lastSignal, lastAction, lastLedger, pnlEstimate }, GET /api/admin/trading/strategies/:id/logs?limit= -> recent append-only logs and snapshots. Optional POST /api/admin/trading/strategies/refresh (owner-only) to trigger immediate rescan (rate-limited).

Push model: support SSE or WebSocket to stream strategy state updates (signal fired, order placed, fills, slippage) to the UI. Fallback to polling with configurable TTL (default 10s). Server must rate-limit and cache path_find/book_offers results to avoid public node overload.

Strategy state model: canonical fields for each strategy include: strategy_id, name, mode (paper/canary/prod), enabled, targetPairs, currentPosition (token balances), lastSignal { type, score, sourceDataHash }, lastAction { type, txHash?, outcome }, pnlEstimate, riskFlags (e.g., issuer_untrusted, low_liquidity). Persist each update as append-only snapshot tied to ledger index.

Observability & audit: persist all raw snapshots used for decisions (orderbook snapshots, path_find snapshot, account_tx events) to external storage (S3/object store or host data path outside repo). Store strategy logs in immutable append-only format for replay and post-mortem.

Safety & gating: server must load XRPL_WALLET_SEED from secret manager at runtime only. UI must never accept seeds. No signing endpoints exposed unless operator explicitly enables them with multisig/time-delay and manual approval. All endpoints must reuse existing owner authentication and whitelist logic in server.js.

Policy & risk controls in UI: surface per-strategy risk controls (max notional per trade, daily max exposure, issuer allowlist, stop-loss and circuit-breaker states). Allow toggling strategy mode between paper/canary/prod (owner-only) but do not allow enabling live signing from UI.

Metrics & visualizations: show per-strategy key metrics: signal strength timeline, PnL estimate, realized fees, slippage distribution, fills table (ledger-indexed) and last path snapshots. Include a visible network label (TESTNET / MAINNET) and strategy run mode.

Implementation notes for server-side adapters: abstract xrpl client access behind adapters that implement idempotency keys, exponential backoff, caching of book_offers/path_find, and publish sanitized state to endpoints. Use existing server.js auth patterns when adding routes; do not weaken access.

Test & deployment: implement on XRPL Testnet first. Verify GET /api/admin/trading/strategies shows strategies and GET /api/admin/trading/strategies/status reflects simulated trades (paper mode). Confirm snapshots written to configured external storage and that UI displays them. Add CI checks to ensure no secret-material is committed.

entries.title: Admin Project: XRPL Trading Bot UI (admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot)
entries.summary: Owner-only UI spec and implementation checklist for a read-only monitoring dashboard for the personal XRPL trading bot. UI is red/black themed, observation-only (no client-side seeds), surfaces cross-ecosystem metrics (XRPL, Xahau, Flare, EVM sidechain) and links to server endpoints for live data.
details: Target path: admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/ (create new folder; owner-only route). Do not change public top-level files.

Files to create: index.html (page skeleton), client.js (client-side logic only for observation and presentation), style.css (local red/black theme), README.md (developer notes and deploy instructions), assets/ (icons).
Theme: primary colors red (#8B0000) and black; make primary action buttons and highlights red; avoid global style modifications (do not edit top-level style.css).

UI components: connection indicator (green/yellow/red), public wallet address, formatted XRP balance, last synced ledger/time, recent transactions table (direction, amount, ledger, memo), metrics panels (XRPL offers, AMM TVL, Xahau tokens/offer counts, Flare F-Asset balances, EVM pegged asset balances), manual refresh button and auto-refresh toggle.

Metrics to surface (minimum): XRP wallet balance (drops & XRP), recent account_tx list, open book_offers snapshots for watched markets, AMM pool TVL & fee accrual, Xahau listed tokens and on-ledger offers, Flare F-Asset balances (Songbird/Flare), EVM-sidechain balances for pegged assets, selected path_find snapshots and last executed route snapshot, PnL estimate and recent fills summary, slippage & last-execution snapshot.

Data model & UX: present timestamped snapshots, show network label (TESTNET / MAINNET), show data freshness, and present small charts (sparkline) for balance/TVL trends. All action controls are read-only; no signing UI or seed inputs in the browser.
Realtime vs polling: prefer server push (SSE/WebSocket) for account_tx and strategy updates. Fallback to periodic polling with a conservative default (5–10s) and client-side rate-limit guard.
Safety: never accept or display private seeds; show an explicit notice that spending requires server-side secrets and manual owner approval; default UI to TESTNET for development.

Audit & persistence: UI queries server endpoints that expose sanitized snapshots; raw snapshots and append-only audit logs are persisted outside the repo (S3/object-store/local data path configured by env).
Project listing: add an owner-only link entry to the admin-projects index page referencing admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/ (do not modify public site nav).

entries.title: Server Integration: xrpl.js Adapters, Endpoints & Data Pipeline
entries.summary: Server-side design and endpoint contract to safely connect the trading-bot UI to XRPL via xrpl.js, implement owner-only API endpoints, keep secrets out of repo, persist snapshots for audit and provide caching/rate-limit protections.
details: Language & runtime: Node.js server using xrpl.js for canonical XRPL integration. If repo uses another runtime, adapt to xrpl-py equivalent behind same server API contract.
Secrets: XRPL_WALLET_SEED and RIPPLED_URL must be injected at runtime from a secrets manager (Vault/AWS Secrets Manager/GCP). Never log or persist seeds in repo.
Preserve auth: any new endpoints must reuse existing owner authentication and owner-wallet whitelist in server.js. Do not weaken authentication logic.

Server code layout: create lib/xrpl/client.js (xrpl adapter + connection management), services/indexer.js (persisted snapshots and caching), routes/admin/trading.js (owner-only endpoints). Update server.js to mount new routes while preserving auth.

Recommended endpoints (owner-only): GET /api/admin/trading/status -> { address, balanceDrops, balanceXRP, lastLedger, connected, lastSyncTs }, GET /api/admin/trading/metrics -> latest metrics snapshot, GET /api/admin/trading/txs?limit= -> recent txs, GET /api/admin/trading/path?suggest -> last path snapshots, POST /api/admin/trading/refresh (owner-only, rate-limited) to trigger immediate refresh.

Server XRPL flow: on startup load seed from secret store -> Wallet.fromSeed(seed) -> derive address -> connect to rippled client with retry/backoff -> fetch account_info initial balance -> subscribe to account_tx and ledger streams -> persist snapshots and publish sanitized status to owner-only endpoints or SSE.

Caching & rate-limiting: cache book_offers and path_find snapshots for short TTL (e.g., 3–10s) and implement server-side rate-limits for refresh endpoints. Use exponential backoff on rippled errors; protect public nodes from overuse.

Audit & persistence: write append-only logs for each snapshot and API call to external storage (S3/object store or host data path outside repo). Persist ledger-indexed snapshots to enable replay and reconciliation.

No signing by default: server exposes observational endpoints only; signing endpoints may be added later only after operator confirms and only under multisig/time-delay controls with explicit audit logging.
Observability: expose metrics (connection_health, last_ledger, snapshot_latency, offers_tracked_count) to existing monitoring stack and surface critical alerts to on-call/runbook.

Test & deploy: validate on XRPL Testnet first; require secrets injection on staging before any mainnet runs; include automated health-check that verifies GET /api/admin/trading/status returns expected public address and balance snapshot.

entries.title: Repo Changes & Dependencies: Files to Add, Package.json updates and Safe Defaults
entries.summary: Concrete file additions and package dependency recommendations to implement the admin XRPL trading-bot project while preserving workspace guardrails (no secrets, owner-only).
details: Files to add under repo (owner-only area): admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/index.html, admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/client.js, admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/style.css, admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/README.md, lib/xrpl/client.js, services/indexer.js, routes/admin/trading.js, docs/runbook-xrpl-trading-bot.md.

package.json changes (recommended additions): add dependency 'xrpl' (official xrpl.js), add 'ws' or use built-in WebSocket per environment, add 'axios' or use built-in fetch for server->third-party calls, and add a 'build'/'start' script if not present. Example packages: { "xrpl": "^2.0.0", "axios": "^1.0.0" }. Verify existing package versions before editing.
server.js change notes: mount new routes from routes/admin/trading.js and reuse existing owner auth whitelist. Do not remove or weaken existing auth checks.

Storage config: ensure environment variables or config keys for SNAPSHOT_STORAGE (S3 path or local data path outside repo) and WALLET_MIN_BALANCE_ALERT are documented in README.md and runbook. Do not write secrets into .env or repo files.

CI/test guidance: add basic smoke test in ci/ to verify endpoints are owner-authenticated and that GET /api/admin/trading/status returns the configured public address in test mode; include backfill replay test that reads persisted snapshots for deterministic behavior.

entries.title: Admin Projects Index: Add Owner-Only Link to XRPL Trading Bot
entries.summary: Minimal change plan to surface the new admin project in the owner-only admin-projects listing without touching public navigation or protected top-level files.
details: Add an owner-only entry in admin-projects/index.html or the admin-projects listing generator to link to /admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/ with label 'XRPL Trading Bot' and short description 'Owner-only trading bot dashboard (red/black theme)'.
Ensure link is only visible in the owner-authenticated admin-projects view; reuse existing owner-auth checks used by the projects index page.
Do not modify global nav or top-level index.html/script.js/style.css. Do not add any public-facing references.
Include a short note in admin-projects/index README documenting the new entry and its owner-only nature for auditors.

entries.title: Implementation: Owner-Only Wallet Dashboard (admin-projects/wallet-dashboard)
entries.summary: Concrete implementation recipe and minimal file list to show the connected XRPL wallet, connection status, balance and recent transactions in an owner-only admin UI while preserving guardrails.
details: Target path: admin-projects/wallet-dashboard/ (owner-only route per workspace guardrails). Do not touch top-level public files.

Files to create (read-only UI): admin-projects/wallet-dashboard/index.html, client.js, style.css, assets/, README.md (developer notes + deploy instructions). UI must be observation-only (no seed input).

Server endpoints (owner-only, preserve server.js auth/whitelist): GET /api/admin/wallet/status -> { address, balanceDrops, balanceXRP, lastLedger, connected, lastSyncTs }, GET /api/admin/wallet/txs?limit= -> recent tx list (sanitized). Optional POST /api/admin/wallet/refresh (owner-only, rate-limited) to force refresh.

UI elements: connection indicator (green/yellow/red), public address, formatted XRP balance, last synced ledger/time, recent transactions table (direction, amount, ledger, memo), manual refresh button and auto-refresh toggle; display network label (TESTNET / MAINNET).
Data flow: server pushes account_tx/ledger events via SSE or WebSocket for realtime updates; fallback to conservative polling (5–10s) with client-side rate-limiting to avoid node overload.

Safety features: show explicit notice that spending requires server-side secrets and owner approval; add configurable low-balance alert env var WALLET_MIN_BALANCE_ALERT and surface alerts in the UI; disable any client-side signing capability.

Audit: ensure each API call and status snapshot is appended to an audit log persisted to external snapshot storage (SNAPSHOT_STORAGE) for replay. Include ledger index on each snapshot for deterministic replay.

entries.title: Server Adapter: lib/xrpl/client.js (xrpl.js connection & account adapter)
entries.summary: Operational recipe for a small server-side xrpl client adapter that loads secrets at runtime, derives the public address, subscribes to account_tx/ledger streams, and exposes sanitized status to owner-only endpoints.
details: Language/runtime: Node.js using xrpl.js (official client). If repo uses another runtime, mirror the same contract with xrpl-py behind the same server endpoints.

Secrets & env vars (do NOT commit): XRPL_WALLET_SEED (secret, must be in secrets manager and injected at runtime), RIPPLED_URL (rippled WebSocket/RPC endpoint), SNAPSHOT_STORAGE (external storage path), WALLET_MIN_BALANCE_ALERT (optional).

Connection flow (server-side): 1) load RIPPLED_URL and XRPL_WALLET_SEED from secret manager at process start; 2) Wallet.fromSeed(seed) -> derive classic address and validate equals configured public address record; 3) connect to rippled client with retry/backoff and health checks; 4) fetch account_info for initial balance; 5) subscribe to account_tx and ledger streams; 6) publish sanitized snapshots to internal services and owner-only endpoints/SSE.

API contract: adapter exposes async getStatus() returning { address, balanceDrops, balanceXRP, lastLedger, connected, lastSyncTs } and stream events for account_tx; implement short TTL caching for getStatus to avoid excessive rippled calls.
Persistence & audit: emit append-only snapshot events to services/indexer for persistence to external SNAPSHOT_STORAGE. Include ledger index and raw event payload for replay and reconciliation.

Operational protections: implement exponential backoff and jitter on rippled errors, rate-limit path_find/book_offers calls, and cache heavy queries (TTL 3–10s). Never log seeds or write them to repo files or logs.
No signing by default: adapter only observes and reports. Any future signing endpoints must require explicit operator enable, multisig/time-delay controls and high-sensitivity audit logging.

entries.title: XRPL Strategies Catalog: Watchable Strategies & Agent Brain
entries.summary: Concrete, operational trading strategy designs for an owner-only XRPL wallet agent. Each strategy lists preconditions (trustlines, issuer checks), signal generation, execution recipe (book_offers / path_find / AMM interaction), monitoring hooks, and explicit risk controls suited for watchable/admin UI integration.
details: Agent-brain primitives (how strategies plug in): separate Signal Ingestion (orderbook, trades, ledger events) → Feature Store (time-aligned series, snapshots) → Decision Engine (deterministic rules + ML score) → Risk Overlay (per-trade + portfolio gates) → Execution Adapter (idempotent xrpl.js wrapper) → Audit & Replay (ledger-indexed snapshots).

Global preconditions for all strategies: never attempt token operations without a trustline; verify issuer solvency and gateway inflow/outflow signals; perform all signing only server-side with secrets from secret manager and only after manual ops approvals for live signing.

RSI Mean-Reversion (XRP <-> rUSD): Preconditions: trustline to rUSD, issuer allowed; Signals: RSI overbought (>70) to sell XRP for rUSD, RSI oversold (<30) to buy back; Execution: simulate with path_find -> if simulated net meets slippage threshold, place capped offer/payment with explicit path; Risk gates: per-trade cap (1–3% wallet), cooldown between roundtrips, issuer allowlist check; Monitoring: persist path_find snapshot, chosen offers, fills, slippage, ledger index.

Orderbook Market-Making (issued token pairs): Preconditions: trustlines, whitelist issuers, min depth heuristic; Signals: spread & depth changes, volatility metric; Execution: maintain symmetric offers around mid (book_offers), update on snapshots, use idempotent account_offers updates; Risk gates: max inventory, dynamic spread widening, withdraw on gateway anomaly; Monitoring: quote freq, fills, realized spread, inventory exposure.

Path-Find Multi-Hop Arbitrage / Auto-Bridge Capture: Preconditions: trustlines for route hops, gateway registry; Signals: detected positive simulated arbitrage after fees/slippage; Execution: 1) query book_offers, 2) run path_find simulation, 3) persist path snapshot, 4) attempt atomic sequence (payment with explicit paths/offers); Risk gates: require snapshot locking, min depth on all hops, abort if any offer disappears before submit; Monitoring: simulation vs realized outcome, ledger-indexed traces.

XRPL AMM Opportunistic LP (pool management): Preconditions: AMM pool support, treasury multisig controls; Signals: TVL dip, fee revenue vs historical IL estimate; Execution: deposit/withdraw via AMM primitives with multisig approval if above threshold; Risk gates: max treasury % in LP, multisig/time-delay for large moves; Monitoring: TVL time-series, fee accrual, LP share snapshots.

Gateway Spread Capture & Peg Arbitrage (issued stablecoins): Preconditions: trustlines to stable issuers, issuer health check; Signals: observed spread between gateway on/off-chain rates and XRPL orderbook; Execution: place small crossing offers for immediate fills or use narrow IOC orders; Risk gates: max exposure per issuer, issuer solvency checks, throttle to limit wash-trade risk; Monitoring: gateway inflows/outflows, settlement reconciliation.

Volatility Breakout with Volume Confirmation: Preconditions: robust orderbook tape; Signals: structure break (HH/HL) + volume surge + orderbook depth removal; Execution: laddered limit entries or market with slippage cap; Risk gates: volatility-adaptive position sizing, trailing stop rules, cancel-on-network-instability; Monitoring: entry snapshot, expected vs actual slippage, fills timeline.

Reactive Whale-Flow Hedging & Rebalancing: Preconditions: address labeling & exchange/gateway watchlist; Signals: large inbound to exchange/gateway or large outbound accumulation; Execution: pre-emptive hedge (small %) or rebalance via path_find routes; Risk gates: require multi-signal confirmation (on-chain + orderbook + price), limit number of reactive trades per time window; Monitoring: event correlation logs, outcome reconciliation.

Conservative Portfolio Rebalance Engine: Preconditions: inventory tracking for all trust-lined assets and target allocations; Signals: periodic schedule or threshold drift triggers; Execution: use path_find to choose low-slippage routes, break transactions into micro-trades, prefer trusted stable issuer for consolidation; Risk gates: max daily reposition notional, issuer allowlist, fail if slippage threshold exceeded; Monitoring: scheduled snapshots, rebalancing PnL, fee tallies.

Operational & safety controls common to all strategies: run in paper/canary/prod modes, require operator enable flags for live modes, implement circuit-breakers (global + per-strategy), use idempotency keys for orders/offers, persist raw market snapshots (orderbook, path_find) for each decision, append-only logs with ledger index for replay.

Integration notes for Xahau & XRPL specifics: map Xahau marketplace signals (listed token counts, on-ledger offers) into strategy inputs; use ledger events (offer_create/cancel, AMM events, account_tx) as primary ground truth; always persist pre-flight path snapshots when path_find is used to select routes.

Observability fields to record per decision: strategy_id, mode, signal_timestamp, signal_type, input_hash (snapshot id), simulated_output, chosen_route (path_find/book_offers), tx_hash (if executed), fill_details (ledger-indexed), realized_slippage, pnl_delta, risk_flags, ledger_index.

entries.title: Strategy Watcher UI & Server Contract: Watchable Strategies for Owner Dashboard
entries.summary: Owner-only UI and server endpoint contract to observe and audit active XRPL strategies. Designed for admin-projects read-only interfaces and to integrate with the agent brain; preserves guardrails (no client-side seeds, owner-only routes, append-only snapshots).
details: UI placement & access: host watch pages under admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/strategies/ (owner-only). UI is read-only unless operator explicitly enables live signing server-side under multisig/time-delay policies.

Per-strategy display model: each strategy card must show strategy_id, name, mode (paper/canary/prod), enabled boolean, lastSignal { ts, type, score, inputSnapshotId }, lastAction { ts, type, txHash?, outcome }, pnlEstimate, riskFlags and small sparklines for balance/TVL trends.

Required server endpoints (owner-only, reuse server.js auth): GET /api/admin/trading/strategies -> descriptors, GET /api/admin/trading/strategies/status -> live states array, GET /api/admin/trading/strategies/:id/logs?limit= -> append-only logs, GET /api/admin/trading/snapshots/:id -> raw snapshot payload, optional POST /api/admin/trading/strategies/refresh (rate-limited) to trigger re-evaluation in the agent brain.

Event push model: prefer SSE/WebSocket to stream strategy events (signal fired, order placed, fill, slippage) to UI. Fallback to conservative polling (default 10s) with server-side caching of heavy calls (book_offers/path_find).

Snapshot & audit contract: every decision must persist an append-only snapshot object: { snapshotId, ledgerIndex, timestamp, rawInputs (orderbook, path_find result), simulatedOutput, chosenAction }, and snapshot retrieval must be available via GET /api/admin/trading/snapshots/:id for replay.

Safety & secrets: UI must never accept seeds; server loads XRPL_WALLET_SEED from secret manager at runtime only. No signing endpoints until explicit owner workflow is implemented (multisig/time-delay + manual approval).

Monitoring & alerts integration: surface critical riskFlags (issuer_untrusted, gateway_anomaly, low_liquidity, circuit_breaker_triggered) in UI and push alerts to on-call. Include ability to mark events for manual review/annotation in the audit log.

Implementation notes for server adapters: adapt lib/xrpl/client.js to publish sanitized snapshots to services/indexer; indexer persists to SNAPSHOT_STORAGE (S3/object-store or host data path outside repo). Cache book_offers/path_find for 3–10s TTL to protect public nodes.

Test & deploy guidance: develop and validate on XRPL Testnet / Songbird; verify endpoints work in paper mode showing simulated fills; include smoke tests to assert no secret material appears in endpoints or logs.
